rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Calendar for Thursday, March 13, 2025 Line 1. PETITIONER PAII, INC. Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award. Petitioner PAII, Inc.'s unopposed Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award is GRANTED. Good cause appearing, the Petition to Confirm the Arbitrators' Award is granted and the January 13, 2025, arbitration award is confirmed in its entirety. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Calendar for Thursday, March 13, 2025 Line 8. DEFENDANTs LUKE OBRIEN, MEL MURPHY, BRANDON BROWN, CDXX LLC, HAPPYLAND LLC,and ST. COMAS LLC.'s Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings. Continued to April 1, 2025. Opposing party is ordered to provide courtesy copies immediately in compliance with SF Local Rule 2.7B. The court notes that any party can obviate the need for a continuance of a motion for lack of courtesy copies by simply lodging a complete set of courtesy copies of all documents related to the motion.
